calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Apr-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emily Collins, Esq., Hunt & Henriques, 408-360-8485, appearing for Plaintiff;(CourtCall)
Lauren B. Veggian, Esq., The Cardoza Law Corporation, 619-483-1198, appearing for Third Party Defendant David Farber; (CourtCall)

The matter is uncontested. The matter is discussed and the Court rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DAVID FARBE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint.

Nonparty David Farber's motion to quash service of summons and complaint is denied. Although the complaint identifies the name of the defendant as David Farbe, a slight misspelling does not render service defective if the person served is aware that he is the person named as a defendant. (Sakaguchi v. Sakaguchi (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 852, 857.) Here, the proof of service shows that Mr. Farber was personally served with the summons and complaint and confirmed his identity to the process server. Mr. Farber's contention that he was the victim of identity theft and is not responsible for the debt is not a proper subject of a motion to quash, and may be raised on a dispositive motion. Both parties are directed to the provisions of the Identity Theft Resolution Act concerning a debt collector's responsibilities upon receiving specified information from a debtor that he or she is a victim of identity theft. (See Civ. Code Sec. 1788.18.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Rebecca Tovar CSR#14200, email: rtovar@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)
